name: Multidrug Resistance Mutation (MDR1/ABCB1)
description: The MDR1 (ABCB1) mutation causes a defective P-glycoprotein pump in the blood-brain barrier, allowing normally safe drugs (ivermectin, loperamide, certain chemotherapy agents) to accumulate in the brain to toxic levels. Affected dogs can die from routine doses.
treatment_overview: No treatment for the mutation itself. Management involves identifying mutation status via DNA test and avoiding all MDR1-sensitive drugs. If intoxication occurs, supportive care and lipid emulsion therapy may help.
prevention_tips: DNA test all herding breeds before drug administration
Never use ivermectin at anti-parasitic doses in untested herding breeds
